speaker: GOWER
text: Thus time we waste, and long leagues make short,

speaker: GOWER
text: Sail seas in cockles, have and wish but for ’t,

speaker: GOWER
text: Making to take our imagination

speaker: GOWER
text: From bourn to bourn, region to region.

speaker: GOWER
text: By you being pardoned, we commit no crime

speaker: GOWER
text: To use one language in each several clime

speaker: GOWER
text: Where our scenes seems to live. I do beseech you

speaker: GOWER
text: To learn of me, who stand in the gaps to teach you

speaker: GOWER
text: The stages of our story. Pericles

speaker: GOWER
text: Is now again thwarting the wayward seas,

speaker: GOWER
text: Attended on by many a lord and knight,

speaker: GOWER
text: To see his daughter, all his life’s delight.

speaker: GOWER
text: Old Helicanus goes along. Behind

speaker: GOWER
text: Is left to govern it, you bear in mind,

speaker: GOWER
text: Old Escanes, whom Helicanus late

speaker: GOWER
text: Advanced in time to great and high estate.

speaker: GOWER
text: Well-sailing ships and bounteous winds have brought

speaker: GOWER
text: This king to Tarsus—think his pilot thought;

speaker: GOWER
text: So with his steerage shall your thoughts go on—

speaker: GOWER
text: To fetch his daughter home, who first is gone.

speaker: GOWER
text: Like motes and shadows see them move awhile;

speaker: GOWER
text: Your ears unto your eyes I’ll reconcile.

speaker: GOWER
text: See how belief may suffer by foul show!

speaker: GOWER
text: This borrowed passion stands for true old woe.

speaker: GOWER
text: And Pericles, in sorrow all devoured,

speaker: GOWER
text: With sighs shot through and biggest tears o’ershowered,

speaker: GOWER
text: Leaves Tarsus and again embarks. He swears

speaker: GOWER
text: Never to wash his face nor cut his hairs.

speaker: GOWER
text: He puts on sackcloth, and to sea. He bears

speaker: GOWER
text: A tempest which his mortal vessel tears,

speaker: GOWER
text: And yet he rides it out. Now please you wit

speaker: GOWER
text: The epitaph is for Marina writ

speaker: GOWER
text: By wicked Dionyza:

speaker: GOWER
text: The fairest, sweetest, and best lies here,

speaker: GOWER
text: Who withered in her spring of year.

speaker: GOWER
text: She was of Tyrus, the King’s daughter,

speaker: GOWER
text: On whom foul death hath made this slaughter.

speaker: GOWER
text: Marina was she called, and at her birth,

speaker: GOWER
text: Thetis, being proud, swallowed some part o’ th’ earth.

speaker: GOWER
text: Therefore the Earth, fearing to be o’erflowed,

speaker: GOWER
text: Hath Thetis’ birth-child on the heavens bestowed.

speaker: GOWER
text: Wherefore she does—and swears she’ll never stint—

speaker: GOWER
text: Make raging battery upon shores of flint.

speaker: GOWER
text: No visor does become black villainy

speaker: GOWER
text: So well as soft and tender flattery.

speaker: GOWER
text: Let Pericles believe his daughter’s dead,

speaker: GOWER
text: And bear his courses to be orderèd

speaker: GOWER
text: By Lady Fortune, while our scene must play

speaker: GOWER
text: His daughter’s woe and heavy welladay

speaker: GOWER
text: In her unholy service. Patience, then,

speaker: GOWER
text: And think you now are all in Mytilene.
